pr.title: Remove MUST_MATCH from lbfile due to new compiler
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Removed a MUST_MATCH inline assembly fallback for lbFile_800161A0 in src/melee/lb/lbfile.c after a compiler update made the C implementation matchable. The remaining C function calls lb_800195D0 and returns the global cancel flag, guarded by a dont_inline pragma because otherwise the compiler would inline it into callers in the file.
what_changed: Deleted the #ifdef MUST_MATCH assembly implementation of lbFile_800161A0.
Kept the C implementation of lbFile_800161A0 as the only implementation.
Added #pragma dont_inline on around lbFile_800161A0, with the existing #pragma push/#pragma pop used to scope the pragma.
The C function body remains simple: call lb_800195D0(); then return cancel.
smart_moves: Used a targeted dont_inline pragma instead of retaining a full inline assembly MUST_MATCH block.
Scoped the compiler pragma with push/pop so the no-inline behavior is localized to lbFile_800161A0.
Took advantage of the newer compiler's matching behavior to remove handwritten assembly while preserving call boundaries.
decomp_lessons: When a compiler update changes matching behavior, revisit older MUST_MATCH asm blocks; some can be replaced with normal C plus a small pragma.
If a small helper function starts being inlined into nearby callers, dont_inline may be enough to preserve expected codegen and avoid assembly.
Prefer localized pragmas over broad compiler-workaround changes when matching a single function.
naming_conventions: Function names in this file use the lbFile_800xxxxx address-based convention, such as lbFile_800161A0.
The callee lb_800195D0 uses the shorter lb_800xxxxx library naming style.
The global cancel flag is named descriptively as cancel.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed an asm bool lbFile_800161A0() block that manually emitted prologue, call to lb_800195D0, load of cancel from r13, epilogue, and blr.
Replaced the MUST_MATCH conditional assembly path with C source plus #pragma dont_inline on.
The important matching constraint documented in the PR body was preventing lbFile_800161A0 from being inlined into all calls within the file.
follow_up_queries: Find other MUST_MATCH blocks that can be removed after the compiler update.
Search for small C helpers that require #pragma dont_inline to avoid being folded into local callers.
Audit lbfile.c for other compiler-pragmas scoped with #pragma push and #pragma pop.
